# SCPreferencesPath | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ System Configuration ](/documentation/systemconfiguration)

- [ SCPreferencesPath ](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespath)

-  SCPreferencesPath 

API Collection# SCPreferencesPath

## [Overview](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespath#Overview)

The `SCPreferencesPath` programming interface allows an application to load and store XML configuration data in a controlled manner and provide the necessary notifications to other applications that need to be aware of configuration changes.

The functions in this programming interface view the data as a collection of dictionaries of key-value pairs and an associated path name. The root path (”/”) identifies the top-level dictionary. Additional path components specify the keys for subdictionaries.

For example, the following dictionary can be accessed via two paths. The root path (”/”) returns a dictionary with all keys and values. The path “/path1” returns only the dictionary with the “key3” and “key4” properties.



```
<dict>
   <key>key1</key>
   <string>val1</string>
   <key>key2</key>
   <string>val2</string>
   <key>path1</key>
   <dict>
      <key>key3</key>
      <string>val3</string>
      <key>key4</key>
      <string>val4</string>
   </dict>
</dict>

```

Each dictionary can also include the `kSCResvLink` (”__LINK__”) key. The value associated with this key is interpreted as a link to another path. If this key is present, a call to the [`SCPreferencesPathGetValue(_:_:)`](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespathgetvalue(_:_:)) function returns the dictionary specified by the link.

## [Topics](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespath#topics)

### [Creating a New Path](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespath#Creating-a-New-Path)

[`func SCPreferencesPathCreateUniqueChild(SCPreferences, CFString) -> CFString?`](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespathcreateuniquechild(_:_:))Creates a new path component rooted at the specified path in the dictionary hierarchy.### [Associating Information with a Path](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespath#Associating-Information-with-a-Path)

[`func SCPreferencesPathSetValue(SCPreferences, CFString, CFDictionary) -> Bool`](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespathsetvalue(_:_:_:))Associates the specified dictionary with the specified path.[`func SCPreferencesPathSetLink(SCPreferences, CFString, CFString) -> Bool`](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespathsetlink(_:_:_:))Associates a link to a second dictionary at the specified path.### [Getting or Removing Information Associated with a Path](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespath#Getting-or-Removing-Information-Associated-with-a-Path)

[`func SCPreferencesPathGetValue(SCPreferences, CFString) -> CFDictionary?`](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespathgetvalue(_:_:))Returns the dictionary associated with the specified path.[`func SCPreferencesPathGetLink(SCPreferences, CFString) -> CFString?`](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespathgetlink(_:_:))Returns the link associated with the specified path.[`func SCPreferencesPathRemoveValue(SCPreferences, CFString) -> Bool`](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespathremovevalue(_:_:))Removes the data associated with the specified path.## [See Also](/documentation/systemconfiguration/scpreferencespath#see-also)
